campaign.title: How We Create Content That Sounds Like You
campaign.subject: your account team can write your marketing. Here's how to make it actually good.
campaign.description: The content creation process — written by us, approved by you

landing_page.ai_goal: Show consistent content is possible without the business writing it

slides.title: content creation Reality

content.headline: your account team can Write Your Marketing
content.subhead: But there's a right way and a wrong way

slides.narration: your account team can write your marketing content. Blog posts, social media, emails, descriptions—all of it. But here's what nobody tells you: there's a right way and a wrong way to use modern tools for content. The wrong way sounds generic and robotic. The right way sounds like you, but takes a fraction of the time.

slides.title: The Generic Problem

content.headline: Why Most content creation Sounds the Same

problems.result: Vague claims instead of real details

content.example_bad: We are committed to providing excellent service to our valued customers...
content.example_good: We've been fixing cars in Springfield since 1987. My dad started this shop, and we still treat every customer like family.

slides.narration: Why does most content creation sound the same? No context—the modern tools doesn't know your business, so it writes generic content. No voice guidance—it defaults to corporate-speak instead of your personality. No specifics—vague claims instead of real details. No editing—first drafts published as final. 'We are committed to providing excellent service' could be anyone. 'We've been fixing cars in Springfield since 1987, my dad started this shop'—that's you.

slides.title: The Right Way

content.headline: How to Get content creation That Sounds Like You

method.how: Feed it your best content, FAQs, story
Examples of how you talk, words you use
Real names, real numbers, real stories

slides.narration: Here's the right way. Train it on your business—feed it your best existing content, your FAQs, your story. Give it your voice—examples of how you actually talk, words you use, words you don't. Provide specifics—real customer names with permission, real numbers, real stories. Review and refine—spend two to five minutes adding your personal touch. modern tools writes the draft, you make it yours.

slides.title: The 80/20 Rule
slides.narration: Think of it as eighty-twenty. modern tools does eighty percent: research, outline, first draft, variations, formatting, SEO optimization. You do twenty percent: add personal stories, include specific details only you know, adjust the tone, give final approval. Without modern tools, a blog post takes two to four hours. With modern tools, twenty to thirty minutes. Same quality. Fraction of the time.

slides.title: Content Types modern tools Handles

content.headline: What your account team can Write for Your Business

slides.narration: What can modern tools write for your business? Social media posts—daily content, five minutes to review. Blog articles—weekly content, fifteen to twenty minutes to review. Email newsletters—weekly or monthly, ten minutes. Service descriptions—write once, update occasionally. FAQ answers—build a library, five minutes each. Review responses—as needed, two minutes each. Your entire content operation, streamlined.

slides.title: Real Results

case_study.before: 1 blog post per month, 2 social posts per week
case_study.after: 4 blog posts per month, daily social posts
case_study.quote: The modern tools learned our voice. People comment that they love our personality online—they don't know it's modern tools-assisted.

slides.narration: Real example. Home cleaning service. Before modern tools: one blog post a month, two social posts a week, eight hours of work. After modern tools: four blog posts a month, daily social posts, three hours of work. Results: website traffic up one hundred fifty-six percent, inquiries up forty-three percent. The owner told us: 'The modern tools learned our voice. People comment that they love our personality online—they don't know it's modern tools-assisted.'

slides.title: Start Creating with modern tools

content.headline: Ready to Create More Content in Less Time?

slides.narration: Ready to create more content in less time? Set up content creation now—we'll train it on your business, your voice, your specifics. Or see sample content first to see what's possible. Either way, you're about to get your time back while publishing more than ever.

email_1_educational.subject: Content about your business — written by us in your voice
email_1_educational.preview_text: Consistent content without hiring a writer
email_1_educational.body:

You know you should post more. Articles, social updates, newsletters. But who has time?

Here's how we handle it:

→ We learn about your business — your voice, your services, your story

[AM Name]
